definition: The six sense fields through which contact and cognition occur.
notes: The project prefers sense field because it reads more naturally than sense base while still retaining the analytical structure of the term. In dependent origination contexts, the plural force is often strongly implied even when the compound appears as a doctrinal link. The term should be heard as the sixfold cognitive field that makes contact possible, not merely as a static list of organs.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and dependent-origination contexts
When the sixfold structure needs to be explicit in prose
context_rules.notes: Use when the singular English phrase would hide the doctrinal count.

context_rules.context: When the passage highlights the link from name-and-form into the arising of contact
context_rules.notes: Keep the term within the dependent-origination chain rather than treating it as isolated anatomy.

example_phrases.translation: with sense field as condition, contact

example_phrases.notes: Useful for showing how the broader āyatana family works.

example_phrases.translation: with name-and-form as condition, sense field
example_phrases.notes: Upstream link in the standard sequence.
